advantages: Closed-form training: estimate priors, means, and variances in one pass — no iterative optimization.
Very fast inference (log-sum of Gaussian scores) — suitable for edge devices and high-throughput scoring.
Works well on small datasets when per-class feature distributions are roughly unimodal and Gaussian.
Naturally handles multi-class labels with the same recipe — one Gaussian per feature per class.
Outputs class probabilities via normalized posteriors — useful for ranking and threshold tuning.
disadvantages: Conditional independence assumption is often violated — correlated features distort the product of likelihoods.
Gaussian per-feature model fails on skewed, bounded, multimodal, or heavy-tailed features without transforms.
Decision boundaries differ from linear logistic regression — can underfit or misfit when generative assumptions are wrong.
Zero or tiny within-class variance on a feature can explode PDF values unless variance smoothing is applied.
Less competitive than gradient-boosted trees or deep models on large complex tabular data with interactions.
